prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='FT_catboost', seasonality_champion='ONLY_FT_svr', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The validation folds reveal extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.913) and very high seasonality capture variance (1.0717), with FT_catboost leading trend tracking while ONLY_FT_svr dominates seasonal correlation. Early horizon specialists (THETA, ETS, ARIMA) differ significantly from late horizon specialists (NaiveSeasonal, CWT_svr), indicating strong horizon-specific patterns. These conditions strongly support DBA combination to leverage model diversity and reduce variance.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "FT_catboost",
  "seasonality_champion": "ONLY_FT_svr",
  "overall_champion": "ONLY_CWT_rf",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "THETA", "late": "NaiveSeasonal"},
  "tier1_models": ["ONLY_CWT_rf", "THETA", "ETS", "ARIMA", "CWT_rf", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "CWT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_catboost"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_DWT_catboost", "DWT_catboost", "ONLY_FT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "FT_catboost", "rf", "FT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.913,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "down", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The validation folds reveal extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.913) and very high seasonality capture variance (1.0717), with FT_catboost leading trend tracking while ONLY_FT_svr dominates seasonal correlation. Early horizon specialists (THETA, ETS, ARIMA) differ significantly from late horizon specialists (NaiveSeasonal, CWT_svr), indicating strong horizon-specific patterns. These conditions strongly support DBA combination to leverage model diversity and reduce variance."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k5", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35", "ridge_stacking_l250_topk5"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k5": {"top_k": 4}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35": {"top_k": 4, "shrinkage": 0.3}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=9 indicates strong horizon-specific patterns requiring per-horizon selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation). RMSE spread ratio=0.913 and high_seasonality_variance=1.0717 strongly support dba_combination as recommended by pattern_analyst_insights. With n_windows=3, top_k=4 provides stability while inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 adds diversity via weighted averaging. ridge_stacking_l250_topk5 complements with regularization to handle model heterogeneity."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set is diverse: 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35), 1 stacking (ridge_stacking_l250_topk5). No leakage detected - all candidates use safe strategies (baselines don't fit, selection/weighted use past windows only). Diversity rule satisfied: ≥2 different types present. Universe leaderboards show best_single_by_validation and inverse_rmse_weights_k15 variants in POCID leaderboard but current set already has strong coverage. No pruning needed as no single type dominates (max 2 of same type). Score margin top2 is small (0.0075) indicating competitive candidates.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The validation folds reveal extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.913) and very high seasonality capture variance (1.0717), with FT_catboost leading trend tracking while ONLY_FT_svr dominates seasonal correlation. Early horizon specialists (THETA, ETS, ARIMA) differ significantly from late horizon specialists (NaiveSeasonal, CWT_svr), indicating strong horizon-specific patterns. These conditions strongly support DBA combination to leverage model diversity and reduce variance.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=9 indicates strong horizon-specific patterns requiring per-horizon selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation). RMSE spread ratio=0.913 and high_seasonality_variance=1.0717 strongly support dba_combination as recommended by pattern_analyst_insights. With n_windows=3, top_k=4 provides stability while inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 adds diversity via weighted averaging. ridge_stacking_l250_topk5 complements with regularization to handle model heterogeneity.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set is diverse: 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k5), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35), 1 stacking (ridge_stacking_l250_topk5). No leakage detected - all candidates use safe strategies (baselines don't fit, selection/weighted use past windows only). Diversity rule satisfied: ≥2 different types present. Universe leaderboards show best_single_by_validation and inverse_rmse_weights_k15 variants in POCID leaderboard but current set already has strong coverage. No pruning needed as no single type dominates (max 2 of same type). Score margin top2 is small (0.0075) indicating competitive candidates.
